markdown:
#  What are the default 'Scope' options for change events in Procore? 
##  Background 
When creating or editing a [change event](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms) in a Procore project, you can select one of Procore's pre-defined 'Scope' options to classify the scope of the change being considered.
##  Answer 
The table below details the different options in the 'Scope' drop-down list of a change event.  
| In Scope  | 
  * Indicates the cost of the change event is covered within the terms of the original contract.

 |  
| --- | --- |  
| Out of Scope  | 
  * Indicates the cost associated with the change event is not covered by the original contract.
  * When a change event is 'Out of Scope', you will typically create a change order to amend the original contract.

 |  
| TBD  | 
  * Indicates the cost impact of the change event on the original contract has not yet been determined.

 |  
## See Also
  * [What are Procore's default change types and change reasons?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-default-change-types-and-change-reasons)
  * [What gets copied over to a new project from a project template?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-gets-copied-over-to-a-new-project-when-applying-a-project-template)
  * [What is the Procore Analytics Risk Report?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-procore-analytics-risk-report)
title: What are the default 'Scope' options for change events in Procore?
description: Describes the available 'Scope' options for classifying a change event in a Procore project.
